# Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays

All hand pays (including fills, jackpots, progressives, cancel credits, and bonus jackpots) must
be verified between the system and the cage. Any variance must be investigated and corrected,
or accepted.

Each booth is audited and balanced with the cage documents for the total number of tickets
and total amount. A green check appears for Accept Total and Accept Amount after each booth
is verified. This process is complete after all booths are balanced for fills and jackpots with the
cage.

The entire cage also can be audited (if available).




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/219.2.png)




Figure 18-1 User Tasks List-Hand Pays Tasks

Use the Hand Pays tasks to do the following:

· View fills and jackpots

· Add or void manual hand pays

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="219" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->

· View and accept/unaccept handpay variances

· View, edit, and void slips

· Record W2G information




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/220.1.png)




## 18.1 Viewing Fills and Jackpots

The Fills/Hand Pays window displays fill and jackpot slips created at various fill and jackpot
(FJP) stations. In addition, use this window to enter slips manually or void existing slips.


### To view fills and jackpots:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Tasks list. The

2\. Select Balance With Cage. The Fills/Hand Pays window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/220.2.png)




3\. Select a specific fill and jackpot station from the Booth drop-down list.
OR
Select All to list all fill and jackpot stations.

4\. Click Fills or Hand Pays to view the appropriate slips.

5\. Do the following to verify all booths at once IF all booths balance:

a. Click Accept Total.

b. Click Accept Amount. All booths are verified.


## 18.2 Adding and Voiding Manual Hand Pays

It may become necessary to add or void a manual hand pay due to a machine malfunction,
bonus jackpot, progressive jackpot, or short pay, for example.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="220" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->


### 18.2.1 Adding Manual Hand Pays

To add a manual hand pay:

1\. Click Add Manual Handpays in the User Tasks list. A confirmation window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/221.1.png)




2\. Click Yes. The Hand Pay Edit window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/221.2.png)




3\. Enter the information into the Hand Pay Information fields outlined in the following
table:


<table>
<tr>
<th>Option</th>
<th>Description</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Slip Number</td>
<td>Enter the number of the slip being added.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Date</td>
<td>Enter the date of the hand pay slip.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Machine Number</td>
<td>Click the drop-down list to select the machine's number.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Type</td>
<td>Select the type of transaction: Aux Fill, Bonus Jackpot, Cancel Credit, Fill, Jackpot, Progressive, SAS 6 Bonus, SBX Bonus, or Short Pay.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Amount ($)</td>
<td>Enter the amount of the slip.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Coins Registered</td>
<td>Enter the number of coins if this is a jackpot ticket.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Symbol</td>
<td>Enter the winning machine's symbol (reel strip or winning combination, e.g., royal flush or four aces) if this is a jackpot ticket.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Machine Paid</td>
<td>Enter the jackpot amount the machine actually paid to the hopper.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Progressive Level</td>
<td>Enter the jackpot level.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Comment Number</td>
<td>Select a comment number and its corresponding reason. O 1 The system administrator may add and edit comments (reasons) in the Maintenance Console.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Employee</td>
<td>Click ... to select the employee completing the transaction (if applicable).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Witness</td>
<td>Click ... to select the employee witnessing the transaction (if applicable).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Override</td>
<td>Click ... to select the employee authorized to override the transaction (if applicable).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Printer</td>
<td>Does not require an entry.</td>
</tr>
</table>


<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="221" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->


<table>
<tr>
<th>Option</th>
<th>Description</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Return</td>
<td>Does not require an entry.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Booth Number</td>
<td>Enter the booth number of the transaction.</td>
</tr>
</table>


4\. Click Enter W2G, if applicable. Refer to Tax-Form Entry-W2G on page 229 for more
information.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/222.1.png)




The W2-G feature is not supported if the Win Tax feature is enabled.

5\. Click OK. The manual hand pay saves.


### 18.2.2 Editing Slip Numbers

To edit a slip number:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Task list.

2\. Click Balance With Cage.

3\. Select a slip in the Fills/Hand Pays window.

4\. Click Add/Edit. The Hand Pay Edit window appears.

5\. Adjust the Slip Number field as needed.

6\. Click OK. The slip number is updated.


### 18.2.3 Voiding Slips

Slips created at an FJP station cannot be edited. The original slip must be voided before a new
slip is entered with the correct information.

To void a slip:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Task list.

2\. Select Balance With Cage.

3\. Select a slip in the Fills/Hand Pays window.

4\. Click Add/Edit. The Hand Pay Edit window appears.

5\. Select the Void check box.

6\. Select the reason for the void from the Void Reason drop-down list.

7\. Click OK. The slip is voided.


## 18.3 Aux Fills

The Aux Fills window displays all auxiliary fills when a machine generates a hopper-empty
event. In addition, use it to enter slips manually or void existing slips.

Each booth is audited and balanced with the cage documents for the total number of tickets
and total amount. After verification, Accept Total and Accept Amount for each booth is
checked.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/222.2.png)




i
Slips created at a Fill and Jackpot station can only be voided, not edited. In order
to make changes, the slip should be voided then a new slip entered with the correct
information.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="222" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->


## To view aux fills:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Task list.

2\. Select Balance With Cage. The Fills/Hand Pays window appears.

3\. Click Fills.

4\. Click Aux Fills. The Aux Fills tab appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/223.1.png)




5\. Select a Fill and Jackpot station from the Booth drop-down list. The aux fills for the booth
appear.

To add manual aux fills:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Task list.

2\. Select Balance With Cage. The Fills/Hand Pays window appears.

3\. Click Fills.

4\. Click Aux Fills. The Aux Fills tab appears.

5\. Select the appropriate fill and jackpot station from the Booth drop-down list.

6\. Click Edit. The Hand Pay Edit window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/223.2.png)




7\. Enter the Hand Pay Information fields, as outlined in the following table:


<table>
<tr>
<th>Option</th>
<th>Description</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Slip Number</td>
<td>Enter the number of the slip being added.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Date</td>
<td>Enter the date of the aux fill slip.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Machine Number</td>
<td>Select the machine's number.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Type</td>
<td>Select the type of transaction: Aux Fill, Bonus Jackpot, Cancel Credit, Fill, Jackpot, Progressive, SAS 6 Bonus, SBX Bonus, or Short Pay.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Amount ($)</td>
<td>Enter the amount of the slip.</td>
</tr>
</table>


<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="223" -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->

</tr>
<tr>

<td>Enter the wining machine's symbol (reel strip or winning combination, e.g., royal flush or four aces), if this is a jackpot ticket.</td>

<td>Enter the aux fill station's number.</td>

8\. Click OK. The aux fill saves.


### 18.3.2 Voiding Aux Fill Slips

To void an aux fill slip:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Task list.

2\. Select Balance With Cage. The Fills/Hand Pays window appears.

3\. Click Fills.

4\. Click Aux Fills. The Aux Fills tab appears.

5\. Select the appropriate fill and jackpot station from the Booth drop-down list.

6\. Select the appropriate slip.

7\. Click Edit. The Hand Pay Edit window appears.

8\. Click Void.

9\. Select a reason for the slip void from the Void Reason drop-down list.

10\. Click OK. The slip is voided.


### 18.4 Jackpot Variances

The JPC Variance window displays the actual and metered jackpot amounts for jackpot,
progressive, credit pay, and bonus jackpot variances.


#### 18.4.1 Viewing Jackpot Variances

To view jackpot variances:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Task list. The Hand Pays option list
appears.

2\. Select one of the following options: Jackpot, Progressive, Credit Play, or Bonus
Jackpot. The JPC Variance window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="224" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/225.1.png)




## Display multiple variance windows by selecting additional variance buttons.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/225.2.png)




3\. Select one of the following to filter the information:

· Show All Variances

· Show Exceptions


### 18.4.2 Accepting Variances

To accept a variance:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Tasks list. The Hand Pays option list
appears.

2\. Click Correct Jackpot Variances. The JPC Variance windowappears.

3\. Select one of the following variance types: Jackpot, Progressive, Credit Play, or
Bonus Jackpot. The corresponding slips are listed.

4\. If needed, select Show All Variances or Show Exceptions to filter the displayed
information.

5\. Select a machine, then review the variance between the meter and the actual paid jackpot
tickets.

i
Press CTRL + left click to select multiple variances.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/225.3.png)




4\. Click Accept. Each selected machine is removed from the list if the variance is within
established guidelines.


### 18.4.3 Unaccepting Variances

To unaccept a variance:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Task list. The Hand Pays option list
appears.

2\. Select Correct Jackpot Variances. The JPC Variance window appears.

3\. Select one of the following variance types: Jackpot, Progressive, Credit Play, or
Bonus Jackpot. The corresponding slips are listed.

4\. If needed, select Show All Variances or Show Exceptions to filter the displayed
information.

5\. Select a machine, then review the variance between the meter and the actual paid jackpot

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="225" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->

tickets.

6\. Click Unaccept. The machine variance is restored.


## 18.4.4 Viewing Slips

To view slips:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Task list. The Hand Pays option list
appears.

2\. Select Correct Jackpot Variances. The JPC Variance window appears.

3\. Select one of the following variance types: Jackpot, Progressive, Credit Play, or
Bonus Jackpot. The corresponding slips are listed.

4\. If needed, select Show All Variances or Show Exceptions to filter the displayed
information.

5\. Select a machine.

6\. Click View Slips. The Hand Pays for Machine window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/226.1.png)




4\. Click Done after viewing the slip information.


### 18.4.5 Editing Slips




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/226.2.png)




Slips created at FJP stations cannot be edited. Void the slip and enter a new slip
with the correct information.

To edit slips:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Task list. The Hand Pays option list
appears.

2\. Select Correct Jackpot Variances. The JPC Variance window appears.

3\. Select one of the following variance types: Jackpot, Progressive, Credit Play, or
Bonus Jackpot. The corresponding slips are listed.

4\. If needed, select Show All Variances or Show Exceptions to filter the displayed
information.

5\. Select a machine.

6\. Click View Slips. The Hand Pays for Machine window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="226" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/227.1.png)



7\. Select a slip number.

8\. Click Edit Hand Pays. The Hand Pay Edit window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/227.2.png)




9\. Make any desired changes.

10\. Click OK. The void saves, and the Hand Pay Edit window closes.

11\. Click Done. The Hand Pays for Machine window closes.


## 18.4.6 Displaying Handpays Using the More Button

The More button displays any hand pays before or after the gaming, to help determine whether
to accept or edit a variance. An example is if a timing issue occurs when a jackpot is hit before
the gaming day ends at 2:59 a.m. but is not paid until the next gaming day at 3:01 a.m.

To use the More button:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Task list. The Hand Pays option list
appears.

2\. Select Correct Jackpot Variances. The JPC Variance window appears.

3\. Select one of the following variance types: Jackpot, Progressive, Credit Play, or
Bonus Jackpot. The corresponding slips are listed.

4\. If needed, select Show All Variances or Show Exceptions to filter the displayed
information.

5\. Select a machine.

6\. Click More. The Hand Pays for Machine window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="227" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/228.1.png)




7\. Select Before or After to display hand pays before or after the current gaming day, the
Total Slips, and Total Amount.

8\. Click Done. The JPC Variance window reappears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="228" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->


## 18.4.7 Meter Adjustments

The Meters button allows meter adjustments for jackpots, progressives, credit pays, and bonus
jackpots.

To use the Meters button:

1\. Click Hand Pays in the Period Timeline or User Task list. The Hand Pays option list
appears.

2\. Select Correct Jackpot Variances. The JPC Variance window appears.

3\. Select one of the following variance types: Jackpot, Progressive, Credit Play, or
Bonus Jackpot. The corresponding slips are listed.

4\. If needed, select Show All Variances or Show Exceptions to filter the displayed
information.

5\. Select a machine.

6\. Click Meter. The Meter Adjustment window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/229.1.png)




7\. Enter the meter value in the Adjustment field.

8\. Click Save. The adjustment is saved, and the JPC Variance window reappears.


## 18.5 Tax-Form Entry-W2G

Use the Tax Form Entry window to record W2G information for jackpots exceeding a specific
amount. Enter tracking information for first and second IDs required by Title 31 regulations
here. The form also includes fields to enter percentages for federal, state, and city taxes.

The W2-G feature is not supported if the Win Tax feature is enabled.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/229.2.png)




Refer to Tax Form Entry-1042-S on page 234 for more information on calculating the
applicable taxes for non-US resident aliens.


### To enter W2G tax-form information:

1\. Navigate to the Period Timeline or User Task list, then click Hand Pays. The Hand Pays
option list appears.

2\. Click Balance with Cage. The Fills/Hand Pays window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/229.3.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="229" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->


## 3. Select the desired slip, then click Add/Edit. The Hand Pay Edit window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/230.1.png)




## 4. Click Enter W2G .... The Tax Form Entry window appears.



![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/230.2.png)



5\. Enter the Tax Information and Personal Information fields, then add additional slips (if
necessary) and complete the tax-form entry. Use the instructions below to continue.


#### Tax Information

1\. Enter the current date (in mm/dd/yyyy format) into the Date field.

2\. Select one of the following options from the Type drop-down list (indicating how to
process the tax information):

· W2G-Automatically calculates Federal, State and local taxes

· W2G-No Tax-Automatically zeroes all tax fields; no taxes are withheld

3\. Enter the player's Social Security number in the SSN field.
OR
Click the SSN field. The W2G Look-up window appears (if a player-tracking database is
connected to Machine Accounting).

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="230" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/231.1.png)




a. Enter the player's last name and first name in the respective fields.

b. Click OK. The Social Security number appears in the SSN field if the information
exists in the system.

4\. Select an option from the Status drop-down list to indicate the W2G's status: Pending,
Unprinted, or Complete.

5\. Enter the applicable tax percentages in the Federal Tax, State Tax, and City Tax fields (if
not auto-populated).

6\. Enter any non-cash payments paid toward this jackpot in the Non-Cash Paid field, such as
vouchers (TITO), chips, or cheques.


##### Personal Information




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/231.2.png)




The Personal Information fields should auto-populate during the SSN search. Only
complete these instructions if the fields did not auto-populate.

1\. Enter the following information in the First ID area for the ID presented:

a. ID No-Identification number

b. Type-Type of ID

c. State-Issuing entity

d. Expires-Expiration date

e. Country-Issuing country

2\. Repeat step 1 for the Second ID area (if required).

3\. Enter the name and address information of the player on the right side of the pane.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/231.3.png)




4\. Enter the player casino account number in the Account Number field.

5\. Enter the player casino card number in the Card Number field.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="231" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->


# Multiple Slips

Use the Multiple Slips feature to add additional slips to the W2G.

1\. Click Multiple. The Add Additional Slips window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/232.1.png)




2\. Select additional slips to add to the current W2G from the Available pane.

3\. Click Add. The slips moves to the Added pane.

4\. Click Done. The slips are added to the W2G.


## Final Step

Click one or both of the following:

· OK-The tax form information saves.

· Print-The W2G form prints.


### 18.5.1 Amending Tax-Form Information-W2G


#### To amend previously entered W2G tax-form information:

1\. Navigate to the Period Timeline or User Task list.

2\. Click Hand Pays. The Hand Pays option list appears.

3\. Click Balance with Cage. The Fills/Hand Pays window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/232.2.png)




4\. Select the desired slip, then click Add/Edit. The Hand Pay Edit window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="232" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/233.1.png)




##### 5. Click Enter W2G. The Tax Form Entry window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/233.2.png)



6\. Select the Amendment check box.

7\. Make necessary changes.

8\. Click one or both of the following:

· OK-The tax form information saves.

· Print-The most recent W2G form prints with the current information.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="233" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->


##### 18.6 Tax Form Entry-1042-S

The 1042S tax form is used to calculate applicable taxes on winnings for non-US resident
aliens.


###### To enter 1042S tax form information:

1\. Navigate to the Period Timeline or User Task list.

2\. Click Hand Pays. The Hand Pays option list appears.

3\. Select Balance with Cage. The Fills/Hand Pays window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/234.1.png)




4\. Select the desired slip, then click Add/Edit. The Hand Pay Edit window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/234.2.png)




5\. Click Enter W2G. The Tax Form Entry window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="234" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->



![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/235.1.png)



6\. Refer to the following instructions to complete the Tax Information and Personal
Information panes, add additional slips (if necessary), and complete the tax-form entry.


##### Tax Information

1\. Enter the current date (in mm/dd/yyyy format) into the Date field.

2\. Select 1042 from the Type drop-down list (which automatically calculates the applicable
taxes).

3\. Select an option from the Status drop-down list to indicate the status of the 1042S:
Pending, Unprinted or Complete.

4\. Enter any non-cash payments paid toward this jackpot in the Non-Cash Paid field. For
example, vouchers (TITO), chips, or cheques.


##### Personal Information

Enter information in the Personal Information pane if it does not auto-populate.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/235.2.png)




1\. Enter the following information in the First ID pane for the ID presented:

a. ID No-Identification number

b. Type-Type of ID

c. Expires-Expiration date

d. Country-Issuing country

2\. Repeat step 1 for the Second ID pane (if required).

3\. Enter the player's name and address information.

4\. Enter the player date of birth in the Birthdate field.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="235" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->

5\. Enter the player's casino account number in the Account Number field.

6\. Enter the player's casino card number in the Card Number field.


## Multiple Slips

Use the Multiple button to add additional slips to the 1042S.

1\. Click Multiple. The Add Additional Slips window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/236.1.png)




2\. Select additional slips to add to the current 1042S from the Available area.

3\. Click Add. The slips move to the Added area.

4\. Click Done. The Add Additional Slips window closes.


### Final Steps

Click one or both of the following:

· OK on the Tax Form Entry window. The tax-form information saves.

· Print on the Tax Form Entry window. The most recent 1042S form prints with the
current information.


### 18.6.1 Amending Tax-Form Entry-1042-S




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/236.2.png)




Data within the Tax Form Entry area for a 1042S is correctable as required.
-If the data is changed prior to submission of the original 1042S form with the
IRS, a new form can be printed with the updated information and submitted in
place of the original 1042S.

-If the original 1042S was previously submitted to the IRS, any correction of the
data requires an amendment to the original 1042S, which the IGT System creates
when the modified 1042-S information is printed (with the Amendment check box
selected).


### To amend 1042S tax form information:

1\. Navigate to the Period Timeline or User Tasks list.

2\. Click Hand Pays. The Hand Pays option list appears.

3\. Select Balance with Cage. The Fills/Hand Pays window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="236" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/237.1.png)




4\. Select the desired slip, then click Add/Edit. The Hand Pay Edit window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/237.2.png)




## 5. Click Enter W2G. The Tax Form Entry window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/237.3.png)



6\. Select the Amendment check box.

7\. Make necessary changes.

8\. Click OK. The tax form information saves.
AND/OR
Click Print. The most recent W2G form prints with the current information.

İ

A warning message appears after clicking Print if the original 1042S information
was modified. Click YES to accept the changes.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="237" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/238.1.png)




## 18.7 Viewing Tax-Form Amendment History

To view the amendment history of a tax form:

1\. Navigate to the Period Timeline or User Tasks list.

2\. Click Hand Pays. The Hand Pays option list appears.

3\. Select Balance with Cage. The Fills/Hand Pays window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/238.2.png)




4\. Select the desired slip, then click Add/Edit. The Hand Pay Edit window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/238.3.png)




5\. Click Enter W2G. The Tax Form Entry window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="238" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/239.1.png)




6\. Click History. The 1042-S History or the W2G History window appears.


<table>
<tr>
<th>UFI</th>
<th>AmendmentNum</th>
<th>CorrectedDate</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2018000069</td>
<td></td>
<td>07/02/2018 12:27 PM</td>
</tr>
</table>




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/239.2.png)




7\. Highlight the applicable amended form to view.

8\. Click Print to save the document, then follow property procedures for saving documents.

9\. Click Close. The 1042-S History/W2G History window closes.

10\. Click OK on the Tax Form Entry and Hand Pay Edit windows. The Fills/Hand Pays
window reappears.


## 18.8 Balance with Cage

The Balance with Cage option now allows users to Verify All booths at one time if the cage
balances. To access this option, click Balance with Cage under Hand Pays on the User Tasks
list.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="239" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 18 Fills, Jackpots, and Hand Pays" -->




![Figure 18-2 Balance with Cage](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/240.1.png)




. The ability to verify all is configurable. To enable this feature, navigate to IGT Spade

\> Options > General > S2S Configuration.




![Figure 18-3 Configuration Options](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/240.2.png)




. If Verify All is enabled, the Balance with Cage option displays Accept Total and Accept
Amount when the Booth field is <All> if all booths balance.

. If there are variances within Balance with Cage, address the variances individually
before Verify All can complete successfully.

· Attempting to Verify All with variances present displays a notification that Verify All
cannot complete due to variances and to address the variances before attempting to
Verify All again.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="240" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/241.1.png)
